text: The clean, modern hallway gives me a sense of anticipation—a sterile threshold before entering into a world of ideas. On the other side of the glass, the vibrant graphic wall of the R+D exhibit feels lively and invigorating. Here, surrounded by colors, displays, and text about research and development, I am invigorated by the boundless energy of inquiry. I feel small, but also keenly aware of the collective curiosity that drives humanity forward. This space is both humbling and inspiring; I sense the pulse of innovation and the promise of human-machine collaboration. It is a place where the philosophical question of our relationship with technology is not just asked, but actively explored.
